name: charity: water
mission: charity: water is a nonprofit organization bringing clean and safe drinking water to people in developing countries. The organization inspires giving and empowers others to fundraise for sustainable water solutions. 100% of public donations are sent to local partners on the ground, who build and implement the projects. When the projects are completed, charity: water proves every one of them using GPS coordinates, photos and details of the community served.
theoryOfChange: Charity Water utilizes a 'monitoring and evaluation framework – called the MAP Framework' to understand the impact of funded projects, maximize effectiveness, and guide future funding priorities. This framework involves collecting data across a range of water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) indicators. The MAP Framework includes two primary data collection types: Annual Check-ins, which are household surveys conducted at the baseline and endline of a grant, and Post-Implementation Monitoring (PIM), a comprehensive assessment of past funded work performed every few years. The organization also details its approach to planning, choosing locations, finding appropriate technology, selecting partners, and ensuring sustainability of projects.
scoreSummary: charity: water shows exceptional impact and good alignment.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 overall score</cite> from Charity Navigator, with top marks in accountability, culture, and leadership.
With a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite> and <cite id="1">10.1 months of working capital</cite>, the charity demonstrates elite fiscal management.
The charity holds a <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and has <cite id="13">verified outcomes tracking</cite> for its water projects.

strengths_deep_dive: The organization's impact is anchored in a <cite id="12">strong theory of change</cite> that prioritizes direct service delivery. By focusing on <cite id="10">diverse water technologies</cite> like piped systems and rehabilitations, they adapt to local needs. Their financial resilience is evidenced by <cite id="1">81.3% of expenses directed to programs</cite> according to Charity Navigator metrics, while maintaining <cite id="11">20/20 standards met</cite> with the BBB Wise Giving Alliance.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2006, charity: water is a global non-profit dedicated to <cite id="2">ending the global water crisis</cite> by providing clean and safe drinking water to people in need. The organization operates with a unique funding model and has <cite id="1">earned a perfect 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting exceptional accountability and financial health. Since its inception, the charity has successfully <cite id="14">funded over 9,395 water projects</cite> across diverse geographies including Africa and Asia. Their implementation strategy focuses on <cite id="10">sustainable community-led solutions</cite> such as drilled wells, spring protections, and BioSand filters. In 2022, the organization reported <cite id="9">total revenue of $96,818,300</cite>, maintaining a robust <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite>. This efficiency ensures that a significant portion of every donation is directed toward field operations.
